changelog:
Token usage accuracy fix: stop discarding the real upstream token counts.

What was wrong:
- All four response finalization paths (Claude stream/non-stream, OpenAI stream/non-stream) captured the exact input/output token counts from the Kiro event stream in OnComplete, then unconditionally overwrote outputTokens with a local char-heuristic estimate — throwing away the real number on every single response. Input-token precedence was also inverted: the coarse contextUsagePercentage-derived value (rounded to a percentage) was preferred over the exact upstream count.
- This directly corrupted the usage object we return. Open-source CLIs (Claude Code, opencode, Cline, Codex) read our reported usage verbatim for their context-window bars, cost estimates, and auto-compaction timing — none of them recount client-side or call count_tokens for context tracking — so wrong usage numbers meant wrong context %, wrong cost, and mistimed compaction in every client.

Fix:
- Output tokens: the local estimate now runs only as a fallback (if outputTokens <= 0), so the exact upstream count survives whenever Kiro provides it.
- Input tokens: extracted the precedence into a single pure helper resolveInputTokens(upstream, contextDerived, estimated) applied at all four sites. Order, most to least accurate: exact upstream count -> contextUsagePercentage x context window -> local char estimate. An exact upstream count is never overwritten by a coarser fallback.
- Behavior is never worse than before: when upstream sends no counts we still fall back to the estimate; when it does, we now report the truth.

Tests: added proxy/token_precedence_test.go locking the precedence (upstream wins even when smaller, context-derived used only without upstream, estimate only as last resort, negative-as-absent). go build, go vet, and go test ./... all green.

--- 1.0.10-A1 ---

Login fix + automated model discovery, grouping removal, one-click toggles, and per-request account failover.

Critical fix - admin login:
- The admin dashboard login was completely broken: a JS syntax error in the API-key model-checkbox-grid builder (escaped single quotes inside an already single-quoted string literal) failed to parse the entire inline <script> block, so login() and every other dashboard function were undefined and the login button did nothing. Replaced the invalid escapes; the whole script block now parses and the dashboard works again.

Automated model fetching (no more hardcoded list):
- The model catalog is now persisted as last-known-good (config.KnownModels). On a successful upstream ListAvailableModels fetch the catalog is saved; on restart the in-memory cache is seeded from it so /v1/models serves the real ids immediately instead of a hardcoded guess. fallbackAnthropicModels now derives from the persisted catalog and only uses a minimal bootstrap list on a truly fresh install that has never reached upstream.
- Claude Code dashed ids (claude-opus-4-7) are always emitted alongside the dotted upstream alias (claude-opus-4.7) via the existing mechanical dot->dash derivation, so new minors keep working without a code change.

Removed grouping:
- Deleted the per-API-key Group field and per-account Groups across config, pool, handlers, admin API, and the web UI (the Account Group input on API keys + the Groups editor on the account detail modal). Routing now depends on the model allowlist only. Existing configs that still carry group/groups JSON keys are silently ignored (graceful back-compat). Removed pool/group_test.go; added coverage elsewhere.

One-click toggles:
- Each account card now has a one-click Overage toggle (in addition to the existing one-click Enable/Disable), with an optimistic instant repaint and a matching .btn-warning style. The global Allow-Over-Usage switch in Settings is unchanged.

Per-request account failover + performance:
- New runWithFailover dispatcher: when an upstream call fails BEFORE any byte reaches the client (429/quota, 5xx, or connection error), the request transparently rotates to a different eligible account instead of returning a 5xx while healthy peers exist. Streaming handlers (Claude + OpenAI chat) defer the first SSE frame until the first upstream byte so failover is safe; once streaming starts the response is committed and a later error is surfaced inline. Auth (401/403), payment (402/OVERAGE), and client-cancellation are terminal and never burn extra accounts. Capped at 3 attempts. The Responses (Codex) path keeps single-account behavior.
- Failure accounting is per-request: a request that rotates across N accounts cools each distinct account but counts as exactly ONE failed request (matching pre-failover dashboard semantics); a failover-then-succeed counts as zero failures.
- Pool selection no longer nests the config lock under the pool lock (strategy resolved before locking) — removes a hot-path lock-ordering hazard.
- Raised the Kiro transport MaxIdleConnsPerHost 20 -> 100 (and MaxIdleConns -> 200): nearly all traffic targets one host, so the per-host idle pool now keeps warm keep-alive connections for the whole concurrent working set instead of churning TLS handshakes.
- The per-request [Stats] INFO log is now gated on log level so the success hot path skips the getCredits() lock + arg formatting above INFO.

Tests: added failover classifier + dispatcher tests (retryable vs terminal, single-failure accounting, rotate-then-succeed), pool exclude-set tests, and config KnownModels round-trip tests. All packages green on go test ./....
